response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with 13+ years across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, and genuinely relevant developer-tooling work (test runner Trial, Fluent-Asserts assertion library, CI/CD setup, GitLab/Docker infra, MCP code generators, and Selenium/TDD course instruction). However, this is a people-leadership role (Level II Manager) requiring 2+ years of direct people management and 3+ years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing direct reports, running one-on-ones, conducting performance reviews, or leading organizational change. Senior/Staff IC titles are not the same as people management. Mobile-platform depth is also thin (some iOS/Apple TV maintenance years ago). He clears the technical bar comfortably but the core management mandate is unproven, so he is a borderline case.
strengths: Deep, hands-on developer-tooling and test-automation background (Trial, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD, Selenium courses)
Strong system design and craftsmanship signal: TDD, pairing, trunk-based dev, low-latency micro-services at scale
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, dojos, and multi-module courses, suggesting leadership potential
concerns: No demonstrated people-management experience (no direct reports, one-on-ones, or performance ownership)
No evidence of leading organizational change or formal team/technical leadership roles
Limited and dated mobile-platform experience relative to the role's mobile-ecosystem focus
